serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml bottle of Qingdao Laote Beer, equivalent to approximately 330 g, representing a typical single-serve portion as sold in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beer, regular, all (USDA SR Legacy #14003)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Qingdao Laote is a Chinese lager beer produced by Tsingtao Brewery. No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN for this branded beer. USDA SR Legacy regular beer values (per 100 g) are used as the closest approved analogous source. Qingdao Laote is a light-style lager with approximately 3.3% ABV; values are consistent with standard lager beer composition. Energy and macros adapted from USDA SR Legacy beer entry.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Beer (Atkinson et al., Foster-Powell et al.)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 66 (medium) is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular beer. Qingdao Laote is a standard lager; no brand-specific GI measurement exists. The GI of 66 for beer is used as the closest analogous approved estimate. GL per serving = (66 × 11.7 g carbs per 330 g serving) / 100 = 7.7.

hypertension.guidance: Regular alcohol consumption, even from low-sodium beer such as Qingdao Laote (4 mg sodium per 100 g), can raise blood pressure through alcohol-mediated vasopressor mechanisms; individuals with hypertension should limit intake to no more than one standard drink per occasion and ideally avoid regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic index and alcohol-induced hypoglycaemia risk
type2_diabetes.guidance: Qingdao Laote Beer has a GI of 66 (medium) and provides approximately 11.7 g of carbohydrates per 330 ml serving; alcohol can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms and impair hepatic glucose output, so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume beer only with food and monitor blood glucose carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Qingdao Laote Beer contains negligible fat and saturated fat, regular alcohol consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit beer intake in line with national guidelines (no more than 1–2 standard drinks per day) and account for the caloric contribution of approximately 142 kcal per 330 ml bottle.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Qingdao Laote Beer (青岛老特啤酒) is a Chinese lager beer produced by the Tsingtao Brewery, one of China's oldest and most internationally recognised breweries, founded in 1903 in Qingdao, Shandong Province. 'Laote' (老特) is a colloquial Qingdao dialect term meaning 'father' or 'old man', giving the beer a distinctly local cultural identity. It is a light-bodied, pale lager brewed with water, malted barley, hops, yeast, and rice, resulting in a clean, mildly bitter, and refreshing flavour profile with low bitterness and a crisp finish. The beer is typically served chilled in a 330 ml bottle and is commonly consumed as an accompaniment to Chinese meals, particularly noodle dishes such as Lanzhou beef noodles. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 43 kcal, 0.46 g protein, 0 g fat, and 3.55 g carbohydrates, making it a relatively low-calorie alcoholic beverage. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for regular beer. Clinically, the alcohol content poses moderate concern for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. It is not suitable for individuals avoiding alcohol, including those following halal dietary requirements. In Singapore, it is available at Chinese restaurants and noodle establishments.

meal_context: beverage accompaniment to Chinese noodle dishes

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Qingdao Laote Beer exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy regular beer (SR #14003), which is an approved secondary source. The adaptation is defensible as Qingdao Laote is a standard lager with composition consistent with regular beer. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database for beer as an analogous approved source. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy beer data; beer contains naturally zero fat by composition. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 2. sugar_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy; fermentation converts sugars to alcohol. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for beer. 3. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 and vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 and vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy beer data; these vitamins are not present in beer. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero; beer contains no cholesterol. 4. The food_group has been corrected from 'Grains and staples' (input) to 'Beverages' as Qingdao Laote is an alcoholic beverage. 5. This product is not halal due to alcohol content. 6. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for regular beer; no brand-specific GI measurement for Qingdao Laote exists in the literature. GL per 330 g serving = (66 × 3.55 × 330/100) / 100 = (66 × 11.715) / 100 = 7.7. 7. The cuisine tag 'Noodles' in the input reflects the restaurant context (Lanzhou Beef Noodles) rather than the food itself. 8. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules as this is a Chinese food served in Singapore: English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are provided.
